name: Eggos + Peanut Butter
notes: Add chocolate chips if you want a treat. Toast them a little longer for crispy edges.

name: Bag Salad Kit + Kevin's Chicken
notes: Great packed lunch — toss in a container, keep dressing on the side so it doesn't get soggy. Add spinach, cherry tomatoes, shredded cheese.

name: Frozen Veggie Meal
notes: Annie's, Trader Joe's, whatever looks good. Grab 4–5 varieties so you have options.

name: Scrambled Eggs + Toast
notes: Season however you feel — everything bagel seasoning, hot sauce, fresh herbs if you have them.
recipe: Crack 2–3 eggs into a bowl, scramble with a fork.
Heat a small pan on medium-low, add a bit of butter.
Pour in eggs. Don't touch them for 30 seconds.
Gently push the eggs around until just barely set — they'll keep cooking off heat.
Toast your bread while the eggs rest. Season however you like.

name: Veggie Quesadilla
notes: Great way to use up random veggies. Frozen corn, canned black beans, cheese — whatever's around.
recipe: Heat a pan on medium. No oil needed if you're using a nonstick.
Lay a tortilla flat, add shredded cheese on one half.
Add whatever veggies: frozen corn (thawed), canned black beans, spinach, peppers.
Fold the tortilla over.
Cook 2–3 min per side until golden and melty.
Cut in half. Season with hot sauce, sour cream, salsa — whatever you have.

name: Greek Yogurt Bowl
notes: Add granola, fruit, honey, or nut butter. Whatever sounds good.

name: Pasta + Jarred Sauce
notes: Throw in frozen spinach or canned white beans for extra substance. Season the sauce with garlic powder, red pepper flakes, parmesan.
recipe: Boil salted water in a pot. Add pasta.
Cook until just tender (check the box — usually 8–10 min).
While it cooks, warm the jarred sauce in a second pan.
Optional: stir in frozen spinach or canned white beans.
Drain pasta, add to sauce. Toss.
Season with salt, red pepper flakes, parmesan if you have it.

name: Salsa Verde Chicken Enchiladas
notes: Pre-cooked shredded chicken makes this stupidly easy. White cheese melts better than yellow. Don't skimp on the sour cream.
recipe: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Spread a thin layer of salsa verde on the bottom of a baking dish.
Fill each tortilla: a scoop of shredded chicken, a spoonful of sour cream, a handful of cheese. Roll up, seam-side down.
Pour the rest of the salsa verde over the top. Cover with more cheese.
Bake 20–25 min until bubbly and the cheese is starting to brown.
Serve with extra sour cream.

shopping_items.item: White cheese (Monterey Jack or queso fresco)

name: Ground Beef Tacos
notes: Keep toppings simple — shredded cheese, sour cream, salsa. Corn tortillas hold up better if you warm them in the pan first.
recipe: Brown ground beef in a pan over medium-high heat, breaking it up as it cooks.
Drain most of the fat. Season with cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, salt — go by smell and taste.
Add a splash of water, stir, let it simmer a minute.
Warm tortillas in the same pan or a dry skillet.
Load them up: beef, cheese, sour cream, salsa, whatever else you have.

name: Air Fryer Nachos
notes: Use the air fryer basket lined with foil for easy cleanup. Canned jalapeños, black beans, whatever you have.
recipe: Line air fryer basket with foil.
Spread chips in a single-ish layer.
Add toppings: canned beans, cheese, jalapeños.
Air fry at 350°F for 3–5 min until the cheese is melted.
Top with sour cream, salsa, avocado — whatever's around.

name: Teriyaki Ground Beef Rice Bowl
notes: Good packed lunch — travels well in a container, microwave at work or eat at room temp. Bottled teriyaki sauce is completely fine.
recipe: Cook rice (microwave pouches are great here).
Brown ground beef in a pan, breaking it up.
Drain most of the fat. Pour in a few glugs of teriyaki sauce, stir to coat.
Let it simmer a minute until sticky.
Serve over rice. Add whatever toppings — frozen edamame, shredded carrots, a fried egg.

name: Air Fryer Gyoza / Dumplings
notes: Frozen gyoza straight from the bag into the air fryer. Dipping sauce: soy sauce + a little rice vinegar + chili flakes.
recipe: Spray air fryer basket lightly with oil.
Arrange frozen gyoza in a single layer.
Air fry at 375°F for 8–10 min, flipping halfway.
Mix dipping sauce: soy sauce + rice vinegar + chili flakes, or use store-bought.

name: Ramen + Soft Egg + Veggies
notes: Instant ramen is the base — the egg and veg make it an actual meal. Baby spinach wilts right in.
recipe: Boil water, cook ramen noodles.
While noodles cook, soft-boil an egg (6–7 min in boiling water) or just crack one straight into the broth.
Add seasoning packet (or miso paste + soy sauce instead).
Throw in a handful of spinach or frozen edamame at the end.
Top with the egg, a drizzle of sesame oil if you have it.

name: Air Fryer Tofu + Peanut Sauce Bowl
notes: Packs great for lunch — tofu and peanut sauce are good at room temp. Keep sauce separate if you're packing it ahead. Press the tofu first so it gets crispy.
recipe: Press tofu for at least 15 min (tofu press or wrap in a towel with something heavy on top).
Cut into cubes, toss with a little soy sauce and cornstarch.
Air fry at 400°F for 12–15 min, shaking halfway, until crispy.
Cook rice while the tofu is going.
Toss any veggies (broccoli, edamame, shredded carrots) in the air fryer for the last 5 min.
Assemble: rice, tofu, veggies, peanut sauce drizzled over everything.

name: Buddha Bowl
notes: Perfect packed lunch — assemble in a container the night before, dressing on the side. Grain + protein + veg + sauce. No rules.

shopping_items.item: Microwave grain pouches (rice, quinoa, or farro)

name: Shakshuka
notes: Fancy-sounding, stupidly easy. Canned crushed tomatoes do all the work. Feta on top is not optional. Pita for dipping.
recipe: Heat a pan on medium, add olive oil.
Add diced onion and peppers if you have them — or skip straight to the tomatoes.
Pour in a can of crushed tomatoes. Season with cumin, smoked paprika, salt, garlic powder.
Let it bubble for 5 minutes, then make little wells with a spoon.
Crack an egg into each well. Cover the pan.
Cook 5–8 min until whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Top with crumbled feta, fresh herbs if you have them. Serve with pita.

name: Greek Salad + Hummus + Pita
notes: Great packed lunch — pack veggies, pita, and hummus in a container the night before. No heating needed. Cucumber, olives, cherry tomatoes, feta.

name: Ground Beef + Couscous + Herbs
notes: Good packed lunch — couscous is great at room temp or microwaved. Make a batch, pack in containers for the week. Lemon and cumin make ground beef taste nothing like taco night.
recipe: Pour boiling water over couscous (equal parts), cover with a plate, wait 5 min. Fluff with a fork.
Brown ground beef in a pan. Drain fat.
Season with cumin, cinnamon (just a pinch), garlic powder, salt, lemon zest if you have it.
Serve beef over couscous. Squeeze lemon over everything.
Top with fresh parsley, crumbled feta, or whatever sounds good.

name: Air Fryer Falafel + Pita
notes: Good packed lunch — falafel is totally fine at room temp. Air fry in the morning, pack with pita, hummus, and veggies. Microwave if you want it warm.
recipe: Air fry frozen falafel at 375°F for 10–12 min, flipping halfway.
Warm pita in the air fryer last 2 min.
Load up: falafel, hummus, cucumber, tomato, a drizzle of hot sauce or tzatziki.

name: White Bean Tomato Soup (Crockpot)
notes: Dump everything in, walk away for 4–6 hours. Mash some beans against the side to thicken it. Crusty bread is essential.
recipe: Dump into the crockpot: 2 cans white beans (drained), 1 can crushed tomatoes, 1 can diced tomatoes, broth, garlic powder, Italian seasoning, salt, red pepper flakes.
Optional: add a parmesan rind if you have one — incredible flavor.
Cook on low 4–6 hours or high 2–3 hours.
Mash some beans against the side to thicken it up.
Stir in a handful of spinach at the end. Serve with bread.

name: Avocado Toast
notes: Everything bagel seasoning is the move. Add a fried egg or sliced cherry tomatoes if you want more substance.

name: Cottage Cheese Bowl
notes: Go savory: cucumber, cherry tomatoes, everything bagel seasoning, olive oil. Or sweet: honey, berries, granola. Both are great.

name: Butternut Squash Soup (Crockpot)
notes: Use pre-cut frozen squash to skip all the peeling and chopping. Blend it smooth with the immersion blender. Coconut milk makes it creamy and a little bit fancy.
recipe: Add to crockpot: frozen butternut squash (one bag), vegetable broth (about 2 cups), garlic powder, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, a pinch of cinnamon.
Cook on low 6–8 hours or high 3–4 hours until squash is completely soft.
Use the immersion blender directly in the crockpot and blend until smooth.
Stir in a splash of coconut milk or cream. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Top with a swirl of cream, pumpkin seeds, or crusty bread for dipping.

name: Creamy Potato Soup (Crockpot)
notes: Tastes like a lot of effort, requires almost none. Blend half of it and leave some chunks for texture. Top with shredded cheese and sour cream.
recipe: Chop Yukon gold potatoes into rough chunks — no need to peel.
Add to crockpot with broth, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, pepper.
Cook on low 6–8 hours or high 3–4 hours until potatoes fall apart.
Use the immersion blender to blend about half — keep it chunky.
Stir in a big spoonful of sour cream or cream cheese.
Top with shredded cheese, chives or green onion, more sour cream.

name: Red Lentil Soup (Crockpot)
notes: Red lentils dissolve as they cook — no draining, no blending needed, but the immersion blender makes it extra silky if you want. Very filling, very cheap.
recipe: Add to crockpot: rinsed red lentils (1 cup), broth (4 cups), canned diced tomatoes, garlic powder, cumin, turmeric, smoked paprika, salt.
Cook on low 6–8 hours or high 3–4 hours. Lentils will break down on their own.
Optional: use immersion blender for a smoother texture.
Squeeze in half a lemon at the end — it brightens everything.
Serve with pita or crusty bread.

name: Snack Plate
notes: This is a meal. Grab whatever's around and put it on a plate. The vibe is: something crunchy, something creamy, something fresh, something sweet. That's it.

shopping_items.item: Deli turkey or hard boiled eggs
Hummus or peanut butter for dipping

name: Caprese + Bread
notes: Fresh mozzarella, tomatoes, olive oil, salt. That's really it. A little balsamic glaze if you have it. Bread for soaking up the oil.

name: Bento Box
notes: Literally just fill compartments. Leftovers, fresh veg, dip, fruit, crackers, cheese, hard boiled eggs — whatever fits. The point is variety with zero cooking.

name: Tomato Basil Soup (Crockpot)
notes: Canned whole tomatoes make a better soup than crushed — they hold more flavor. Blend it completely smooth. Cream cheese at the end makes it incredibly silky. Grilled cheese is mandatory.
recipe: Quick chop: half an onion, 2–3 celery stalks. Throw them in the crockpot.
Add: 2 cans whole peeled tomatoes (squish them with your hands as you add them), 1 cup broth, garlic powder, dried basil, salt, a pinch of sugar.
Cook on low 6–8 hours or high 3–4 hours.
Use the immersion blender and blend completely smooth.
Stir in a block of cream cheese (softened) or a splash of cream. Blend again.
Taste and adjust salt. Serve with grilled cheese or crusty bread.

name: Black Bean Soup (Crockpot)
notes: Total pantry dump. Blend about half with the immersion blender so it's thick and creamy but still has some texture. Squeeze of lime at the end is not optional.
recipe: Quick chop: half an onion, 2 celery stalks if you have them. Add to crockpot.
Add: 3 cans black beans (drained), 1 can diced tomatoes, 2 cups broth, cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, oregano, salt, a pinch of cayenne.
Cook on low 6–8 hours or high 3 hours.
Blend about half with the immersion blender — leave some whole beans.
Squeeze in a lime, taste and adjust seasoning.
Top with sour cream, shredded cheese, hot sauce.

name: Corn Chowder (Crockpot)
notes: Frozen corn is perfect here — no thawing needed. The cream cheese at the end makes it thick and rich. Top with shredded cheese, chives, hot sauce.
recipe: Quick chop: half an onion, 2–3 celery stalks, 2 medium potatoes into small cubes. Add to crockpot.
Add: one bag frozen corn, 2 cups broth, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, pepper.
Cook on low 6–8 hours or high 3–4 hours until potatoes are soft.
Blend part of it with the immersion blender — maybe 1/3 — to thicken it up.
Stir in cream cheese or cream until melted and smooth.
Top with shredded cheddar, green onion, hot sauce.

name: Minestrone (Crockpot)
notes: Add the pasta in the last 30 min only — otherwise it gets mushy. Everything else is a total dump. Parmesan on top is the move.
recipe: Quick chop: half an onion, 2–3 celery stalks, a carrot if you have one. Add to crockpot.
Add: 1 can diced tomatoes, 1 can white beans (drained), 1 can kidney beans (drained), 3 cups broth, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, red pepper flakes.
Cook on low 6–8 hours or high 3–4 hours.
In the last 30 min, add a handful of small pasta (ditalini, elbows, orzo) and a handful of frozen spinach or zucchini.
Taste and adjust salt. Top with parmesan.

name: Chicken Wrap
notes: 2-minute packed lunch. Roll tight and wrap in foil so it holds together in your bag. Eat at room temp — no microwave needed.

name: Cold Pasta Salad
notes: Make a big batch on Sunday, pack in containers for the whole week. Gets better as it sits. Eat cold — no microwave needed.
recipe: Cook pasta (rotini or penne work best — they hold dressing). Drain and rinse with cold water.
Toss with Italian dressing while still warm so it soaks in.
Add: halved cherry tomatoes, kalamata olives, crumbled feta, diced cucumber, any other veg you have.
Season with salt, pepper, dried oregano.
Refrigerate. Keeps 4–5 days. Add a little more dressing before eating if it seems dry.

name: Protein Smoothie
notes: Drink straight from the bullet cup — zero dishes. Frozen fruit means no ice needed. Add a handful of spinach if you have it; you won't taste it.
recipe: Add to bullet cup: 1 scoop protein powder, a big handful of frozen fruit, fill to the line with milk.
Optional: toss in a handful of baby spinach — it turns green but tastes like fruit.
Blend until smooth. Drink from the cup.

shopping_items.item: Frozen fruit mix (mango, berry, or tropical)

name: Green Smoothie
notes: Sounds intimidating, tastes like a tropical fruit smoothie. The spinach disappears into the frozen mango. Good way to sneak in extra veggies without thinking about it.
recipe: Add to bullet cup: 1 scoop protein powder, a big handful of frozen mango or pineapple, a handful of baby spinach, fill with milk.
Blend until totally smooth — the spinach takes a few extra seconds.
Drink from the cup.

name: PB Banana Smoothie
notes: Tastes like dessert. Freeze your ripe bananas so you never have to throw one out — just peel and freeze in a bag.
recipe: Add to bullet cup: 1 scoop protein powder, 1 banana (frozen is even better), 1 big spoonful of peanut butter, fill with milk.
Blend until smooth. Drink from the cup.

name: Cuties / Clementines
notes: Grab a bag and keep them out on the counter. The easier something is to grab, the more you actually eat it.

name: Banana
notes: Keep a bunch on the counter. Freeze the ones going brown — they're perfect for smoothies.

name: String Cheese
notes: Keep a pack in the fridge. Grab one when you need something fast.

name: Crackers + Peanut Butter
notes: Any cracker works. Add a few apple slices on the side if you have them.

name: Apple + Peanut Butter
notes: Slice it if you feel like it, eat it whole if you don't. Both count.

name: Mixed Nuts / Trail Mix
notes: Keep a bag in your bag or on the counter. Almonds, cashews, whatever you like. Good thing to grab when you're about to make a bad decision.

name: Microwave Edamame
notes: Frozen edamame in the bag — microwave 3–4 min, sprinkle with salt. Surprisingly filling and really satisfying to eat.

name: Hard Boiled Eggs
notes: Boil a batch of 6 on Sunday, keep in the fridge all week. Grab one whenever. Everything bagel seasoning makes them way better.
recipe: Put eggs in a pot, cover with cold water by an inch.
Bring to a boil, then turn off the heat and cover for 10–12 min.
Transfer to a bowl of ice water for 5 min, then peel or refrigerate in the shell.
Keeps in the fridge for up to a week.
